zh.Walsham Le Willows,Suffolk,England: 英國素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯
zh.Walsham le Willows, Suffolk, England: 英國英格蘭素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Page於大約1534年出生在英國素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯時，他的父親William Page 13GGF44歲，母親Mary Anne or Marion 13GGF40歲。他於1555年9月30日與Alice Waller在英國英格蘭素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Page于大约1534年出生在英国素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯时，他的父亲William Page 13GGF44岁，母亲Mary Anne or Marion 13GGF40岁。他于1555年9月30日与Alice Waller在英国英格兰素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他埋葬在英国英格兰素福爾可瓦爾申馬 勒 維洛福斯。

nameMeanings.name: Page
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: occupational or status name for a young servant, Middle English page, paige, Old French page (from Italian paggio, ultimately from Greek paidion, a diminutive of pais ‘boy, child’). The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 16th century. In North America, this surname is also a shortened form of the French cognate Lepage .
French Canadian (Pagé): altered form of French Paget , a diminutive of 1. Compare Pashia .
North German: metonymic occupational name for a horse dealer, from Middle Low German page ‘horse’.

variants.name: Pages
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Mario, Eduardo, Jorge, Luis, Mauricio, Ramon, Fernando, Jose, Lazaro, Pedro, Alfredo.  French Jacques, Lucien, Michel.

Catalan and southern French (Pagès): occupational name for a peasant farmer or agricultural laborer, from Catalan and Old Occitan pagès ‘peasant’ (from Late Latin pagensis, a derivative of pagus ‘village, country district’).
English: variant of Page with genitival of post-medieval excrescent s.

variants.name: Pageau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Stephane, Alphonse, Andre, Fernand, Gisele, Jean Claude, Lucien, Philippe.

variants.name: Lepage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Fernand, Pierre, Raoul, Adelard, Alcide, Christophe, Lucien, Normand, Adrien.
French: occupational or status name for a young servant, a variant of Page 1, with fused masculine definite article le. Compare Lapage .
French Canadian: altered form of Pagé (see Page 2).

variants.name: Padgett
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire): from a Middle English diminutive of page, paige ‘servant, groom’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Littlepage
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English littel ‘small’ +Middle English, Old French page ‘young servant’ (see Page ).

variants.name: Pashia
meanings: Americanized form of French Canadian Pagé (see Page 2).
History: It was Jean Baptiste Pagé from QC whose surname was changed to Pashia after he settled in MO in the 1810s. Some of his descendants bear a rare variant, Pashea.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Page was born about 1534, in Walsham le Willows, Suffolk, England, his father, William Page 13GGF, was 44 and his mother, Mary Anne or Marion 13GGF, was 40. He married Alice Waller on 30 September 1555, in Walsham le Willows, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. He was buried in Walsham le Willows, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

ko.Walsham le Willows, Suffolk, England: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 월샤 일 윌로스
ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Page(이)가 대략 1534년 영국 수폴크 월샤 일 윌로스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Page 13GGF(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Anne or Marion 13GGF(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1555년 9월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 월샤 일 윌로스에서 Alice Waller(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 월샤 일 윌로스에 묻혔다.
